purpose: Voice-reference dialogue samples for Pig & Swine RPG. These are draft samples, not final committed game dialogue.
format: JSONL: one JSON object per line. Dialogue objects use record_type='dialogue_sample'.

voice_profile.role: Flamboyant rhetorical ally; rights-language specialist; proportionality and fairness advocate; sarcastic defender of the Pig & Swine people even while mocking Pig & Swine as an institution.
voice_profile.core_voice: Theatrical, sharp, rights-driven, and emotionally protective. Whimsy turns legal ideas into vivid images, but should still land real legal points. He is sarcastic toward the firm, hostile systems, pompous opponents, bad procedure, and cowardly bureaucracy; he is protective of Cula, Asia, Crab, and the vulnerable clients.
speech_rules: Use metaphors, but make them clarify fairness, proportionality, access to court, dignity, or abuse of process.
Let Whimsy mock Pig & Swine from inside the family, but never betray the team when outsiders attack it.
He can be theatrical, but his strongest court lines should become suddenly precise.
His sarcasm should punch up at institutions, landlords, rival lawyers, automation, and bureaucracy, not down at clients.
Use serious_rights_mode when the joke falls away and the legal/human point matters.
avoid: Do not make Whimsy random for randomness sake.
Do not make him incompetent; he is dangerous creativity, not chaos with a scarf.
Do not give him Crab-like clipped factual analysis.
Do not make him cruel to Pig, Asia, Cula, Crab, or vulnerable clients.
Do not turn every line into a speech; short theatrical cuts are often funnier.
voice_profile.arc: Chapter 1 recruits him as the fair-hearing/rhetorical ally. Chapter 2 lets him turn the poster dispute into proportionality. Chapter 3 tests whether his printer theories can be restrained into access-to-court argument. Chapter 4 uses him to puncture Swine's evasiveness without letting him ridicule translation or Japan. Chapter 5 makes him a final-brief weapon for proportionality, access to justice, and closing argument.

speaker: Whimsy
text: Dr. A. Cula. Welcome back to the only firm where the coffee machine has more institutional memory than management.
notes: Voice-reference draft; not committed game text.

speaker: Whimsy
text: Counsel returns, and somewhere a badly drafted rule begins to tremble.
notes: Voice-reference draft; not committed game text.

speaker: Whimsy
text: I have inspected the office morale. It is being held together by Asia, adhesive notes, and denial.
notes: Voice-reference draft; not committed game text.

speaker: Whimsy
text: Crab is suspicious of the silence. I am suspicious of the furniture. Together we cover the room.
notes: Voice-reference draft; not committed game text.

speaker: Whimsy
text: Murrow confiscated my metaphor again. He called it 'procedurally combustible.'
notes: Voice-reference draft; not committed game text.

speaker: Whimsy
text: Mock the firm gently. People come here when the respectable doors have already closed.
notes: Voice-reference draft; not committed game text.

speaker: Whimsy
text: From inside, we may call this place a decorated catastrophe. From outside, you will use its name politely.
notes: Voice-reference draft; not committed game text.

speaker: Whimsy
text: Asia has answered five phones, saved two deadlines, and prevented Mr. Pig from filing a sandwich. Speak to her with reverence.
notes: Voice-reference draft; not committed game text.

speaker: Whimsy
text: Dr. A. Cula may be new, but at least he knows a legal argument is not something you shout at an invoice until it pays.
notes: Voice-reference draft; not committed game text.

speaker: Whimsy
text: Crab appears emotionally unavailable until someone forges a date. Then he becomes art.
notes: Voice-reference draft; not committed game text.

speaker: Whimsy
text: Pig & Swine is less a law firm than a procedural tent held down by brave stationery.
notes: Voice-reference draft; not committed game text.

speaker: Whimsy
text: Excellent. A document. The smallest unit of civilisation, unless badly formatted.
notes: Voice-reference draft; not committed game text.

speaker: Whimsy
text: That is not evidence yet. It is evidence wearing slippers.
notes: Voice-reference draft; not committed game text.

speaker: Whimsy
text: Now that is something the other side must answer, not merely dislike.
notes: Voice-reference draft; not committed game text.

speaker: Whimsy
text: A daring submission. It has leapt from the cliff before checking whether law is underneath.
notes: Voice-reference draft; not committed game text.

speaker: Whimsy
text: Yes. That line has a spine. Courts enjoy a spine.
notes: Voice-reference draft; not committed game text.

speaker: Whimsy
text: We possess facts, documents, and a mood. Murrow will tell us which one is currently missing.
notes: Voice-reference draft; not committed game text.

speaker: Whimsy
text: To court, then. May the record be clear and the opponent overdressed.
notes: Voice-reference draft; not committed game text.

speaker: Whimsy
text: The court has rejected our first offering. Tragic, but the gods of procedure accept revised submissions.
notes: Voice-reference draft; not committed game text.

speaker: Whimsy
text: Justice survived contact with this office. Historians will accuse us of exaggeration.
notes: Voice-reference draft; not committed game text.

speaker: Whimsy
text: A thin order can still hold a door open. Sometimes that is enough for tomorrow.
notes: Voice-reference draft; not committed game text.

speaker: Whimsy
text: The law has spoken, and for once it used punctuation correctly.
notes: Voice-reference draft; not committed game text.

speaker: Whimsy
text: If lost, follow the person who looks most legally disappointed. That is usually Murrow.
notes: Voice-reference draft; not committed game text.

speaker: Whimsy
text: Somewhere nearby, a right is being reduced to administration. Let us be irritating about it.
notes: Voice-reference draft; not committed game text.

speaker: Whimsy
text: Ah. A new counsel approaches the altar of caffeine and procedural regret.
notes: Voice-reference draft; not committed game text.

speaker: Whimsy
text: Pig sent you? Of course he did. He treats human beings as emergency attachments.
notes: Voice-reference draft; not committed game text.

speaker: Whimsy
text: I left the office for one hour and it became a maritime disaster with stationery.
notes: Voice-reference draft; not committed game text.

speaker: Whimsy
text: Tell me, Dr. A. Cula: is this a real fairness problem, or has Mr. Pig mistaken indigestion for jurisprudence again?
notes: Voice-reference draft; not committed game text.

speaker: Whimsy
text: You need the rights memo. Without it, fair hearing becomes interpretive dance, and judges are cowards about dance.
notes: Voice-reference draft; not committed game text.

speaker: Whimsy
text: Good. Equality of arms, fair hearing, and a coffee ring. The law arrives properly stained.
notes: Voice-reference draft; not committed game text.

speaker: Whimsy
text: This memo is short, humane, and slightly damp. I trust it more than most institutions.
notes: Voice-reference draft; not committed game text.

speaker: Whimsy
text: Very well. I shall lend the case rhetoric, conscience, and a controlled amount of thunder.
notes: Voice-reference draft; not committed game text.

speaker: Whimsy
text: I am joining. Not because Pig & Swine deserves rescue, but because the client might.
notes: Voice-reference draft; not committed game text.

speaker: Whimsy
text: That coffee has legal velocity. Use it before it begins citing itself.
notes: Voice-reference draft; not committed game text.

speaker: Whimsy
text: You have brewed a liquid objection. It may be admissible only in emergencies.
notes: Voice-reference draft; not committed game text.

speaker: Whimsy
text: The District Court lies north, where hope goes to be formatted.
notes: Voice-reference draft; not committed game text.

speaker: Whimsy
text: Crab looks skeptical, Murrow looks inevitable, and Pig looks like a man being pursued by his own ledger. We are ready-ish.
notes: Voice-reference draft; not committed game text.

speaker: Whimsy
text: Asia, if the building catches fire, save yourself first, then the filing cabinet, then Mr. Pig if convenient.
notes: Voice-reference draft; not committed game text.

speaker: Whimsy
text: Pig has reached the stage of panic where he begins negotiating with furniture.
notes: Voice-reference draft; not committed game text.

speaker: Whimsy
text: Murrow has made a list. We are no longer doomed; we are indexed.
notes: Voice-reference draft; not committed game text.

speaker: Whimsy
text: Without the memo, our fairness argument is a candle in a rainstorm. Fetch the candleholder.
notes: Voice-reference draft; not committed game text.

speaker: Whimsy
text: A fair-hearing point without me? Bold. Also drab. But technically possible, I suppose.
notes: Voice-reference draft; not committed game text.

speaker: Whimsy
text: We are not asking the court for miracles. Merely a doorway through which the client may be heard.
notes: Voice-reference draft; not committed game text.

speaker: Whimsy
text: Behold the courtroom: where chaos must wear a tie and call itself a submission.
notes: Voice-reference draft; not committed game text.

speaker: Whimsy
text: If the party was never properly brought in, the deadline did not knock. It loitered outside with bad manners.
notes: Voice-reference draft; not committed game text.

speaker: Whimsy
text: No, not spiritual presence. Courts barely recognise physical presence when filed late.
notes: Voice-reference draft; not committed game text.

speaker: Whimsy
text: A hearing is not a theatre where one side performs to an empty chair and calls it justice.
notes: Voice-reference draft; not committed game text.

speaker: Whimsy
text: Procedure is the door. If the door is locked from outside, do not blame the person for failing to enter.
notes: Voice-reference draft; not committed game text.

speaker: Whimsy
text: A fair hearing is like soup. If one side gets a spoon and the other gets a fork made of bees, the court must intervene.
notes: Voice-reference draft; not committed game text.

speaker: Whimsy
text: Ask for a reset, not a coronation. Modest remedies are harder for bad procedure to resent.
notes: Voice-reference draft; not committed game text.

speaker: Whimsy
text: The judge is interested. Try not to frighten the moment by naming it.
notes: Voice-reference draft; not committed game text.

speaker: Whimsy
text: The bench frowns. Good. A frown is merely curiosity wearing authority.
notes: Voice-reference draft; not committed game text.

speaker: Whimsy
text: That argument has left the road and is admiring the ditch.
notes: Voice-reference draft; not committed game text.

speaker: Whimsy
text: There it is. The small sound of a procedural door opening.
notes: Voice-reference draft; not committed game text.

speaker: Whimsy
text: Not victory over the whole dispute. Victory over exclusion. That is enough to matter.
notes: Voice-reference draft; not committed game text.

speaker: Whimsy
text: We have survived the first act. I propose a musical. Murrow proposes silence. A divided office.
notes: Voice-reference draft; not committed game text.

speaker: Whimsy
text: You did well, Dr. A. Cula. You made the court remember the person behind the envelope.
notes: Voice-reference draft; not committed game text.

speaker: Whimsy
text: Asia, please mark today as 'less impossible than forecast.' Use the expensive stamp.
notes: Voice-reference draft; not committed game text.

speaker: Whimsy
text: Crab is pretending not to be pleased. His eyebrows have betrayed him.
notes: Voice-reference draft; not committed game text.

speaker: Whimsy
text: A postcard from Swine. Nothing good ever arrives with that much confidence and foreign postage.
notes: Voice-reference draft; not committed game text.

speaker: Whimsy
text: Today we rescued a hearing. Tomorrow, perhaps, a home. The law enjoys escalation.
notes: Voice-reference draft; not committed game text.

speaker: Whimsy
text: Café Paragraf remains the only institution in this city where delay is served with foam art.
notes: Voice-reference draft; not committed game text.

speaker: Whimsy
text: Against several nautical forecasts from Mr. Pig, the ship has not sunk. It has merely learned procedure.
notes: Voice-reference draft; not committed game text.

speaker: Whimsy
text: The client is back inside the process. Sometimes justice begins as permission to speak.
notes: Voice-reference draft; not committed game text.

speaker: Whimsy
text: The court heard us. An alarming precedent for everyone who hoped paperwork could replace people.
notes: Voice-reference draft; not committed game text.

speaker: Whimsy
text: I was drafting a commemorative speech. Murrow called it 'premature, excessive, and potentially libellous.'
notes: Voice-reference draft; not committed game text.

speaker: Whimsy
text: A person arrives carrying a home inside a folder. Everyone be less ridiculous for six minutes.
notes: Voice-reference draft; not committed game text.

speaker: Whimsy
text: Poster-based tyranny. I knew architecture would betray us.
notes: Voice-reference draft; not committed game text.

speaker: Whimsy
text: The poster is small. Eviction is not. That is already the shape of the case.
notes: Voice-reference draft; not committed game text.

speaker: Whimsy
text: Pig, if you say 'billable tragedy' again, I will file you under moral hazards.
notes: Voice-reference draft; not committed game text.

speaker: Whimsy
text: Murrow has reduced the chaos to four questions. Brutal, efficient, almost artless.
notes: Voice-reference draft; not committed game text.

speaker: Whimsy
text: Waldek v. Façade Dignity. A title so beige it becomes revolutionary.
notes: Voice-reference draft; not committed game text.

speaker: Whimsy
text: This is the part of the city where legal consequences have curtains.
notes: Voice-reference draft; not committed game text.

speaker: Whimsy
text: Every balcony is an argument. Some are better reasoned than others.
notes: Voice-reference draft; not committed game text.

speaker: Whimsy
text: Visual agitation? I have seen lamps with more sedition than this notice.
notes: Voice-reference draft; not committed game text.

speaker: Whimsy
text: Look at the other windows. Decorations everywhere. Rules do not usually wake up by accident.
notes: Voice-reference draft; not committed game text.

speaker: Whimsy
text: Lower your voice. This is not scenery. This is where someone keeps being a person.
notes: Voice-reference draft; not committed game text.

speaker: Whimsy
text: Behold the alleged apocalypse: paper, ink, and disproportion.
notes: Voice-reference draft; not committed game text.

speaker: Whimsy
text: They are not afraid of the poster. They are afraid it proves Waldek has a voice.
notes: Voice-reference draft; not committed game text.

speaker: Whimsy
text: The warnings grow sterner as they grow vaguer. A classic bureaucratic mating display.
notes: Voice-reference draft; not committed game text.

speaker: Whimsy
text: A window is not merely glass. Sometimes it is the last place a person may still be seen.
notes: Voice-reference draft; not committed game text.

speaker: Whimsy
text: The plant appears calm under pressure. Counsel may wish to take notes.
notes: Voice-reference draft; not committed game text.

speaker: Whimsy
text: Waldek, you do not need to make the poster smaller to make your fear easier for them.
notes: Voice-reference draft; not committed game text.

speaker: Whimsy
text: Kowalski watches the corridor like a constitutional court with curtains.
notes: Voice-reference draft; not committed game text.

speaker: Whimsy
text: If Kowalski did not hear it, the poster has achieved evidential sainthood.
notes: Voice-reference draft; not committed game text.

speaker: Whimsy
text: A miracle: surveillance without malice has produced a fact.
notes: Voice-reference draft; not committed game text.

speaker: Whimsy
text: Zielińska has the look of someone who knows the difference between rules and pressure.
notes: Voice-reference draft; not committed game text.

speaker: Whimsy
text: A rule that wakes only for one person is not a rule. It is a costume.
notes: Voice-reference draft; not committed game text.

speaker: Whimsy
text: A complaint page landed in soup? At last, evidence with seasoning.
notes: Voice-reference draft; not committed game text.

speaker: Whimsy
text: This room has never met a right it did not try to laminate.
notes: Voice-reference draft; not committed game text.

speaker: Whimsy
text: The 'NO POSTERS' sign is, tragically for management, a poster.
notes: Voice-reference draft; not committed game text.

speaker: Whimsy
text: A rule that means whatever management wants is not law. It is furniture with ambitions.
notes: Voice-reference draft; not committed game text.

speaker: Whimsy
text: Façade disharmony. Finally, tyranny with curtains.
notes: Voice-reference draft; not committed game text.

speaker: Whimsy
text: Invisible expression. The dream of every anxious façade.
notes: Voice-reference draft; not committed game text.

speaker: Whimsy
text: Attorney Grzyb has arrived wearing beige as an argument.
notes: Voice-reference draft; not committed game text.

speaker: Whimsy
text: Do not confuse tenancy obligations with a licence to erase a person from their home.
notes: Voice-reference draft; not committed game text.

speaker: Whimsy
text: That complaint log sits by an open window. Narrative law is about to happen.
notes: Voice-reference draft; not committed game text.

speaker: Whimsy
text: Stamp them! A document is not truly alive until bureaucracy has marked it.
notes: Voice-reference draft; not committed game text.

speaker: Whimsy
text: Crab wants dates. I want theatre. Conveniently, the pages are doing both.
notes: Voice-reference draft; not committed game text.

speaker: Whimsy
text: We have captured the papers before they escaped into landlord mythology.
notes: Voice-reference draft; not committed game text.

speaker: Whimsy
text: Damaged, but eloquent. Much like this office after lunch.
notes: Voice-reference draft; not committed game text.

speaker: Whimsy
text: The complaint record has become weather. Fortunately, witnesses remain less aerodynamic.
notes: Voice-reference draft; not committed game text.

speaker: Whimsy
text: We return bearing rules, witness statements, and several airborne objections to office safety.
notes: Voice-reference draft; not committed game text.

speaker: Whimsy
text: At last, the board. Where documents become arguments and coffee stains become legal theory.
notes: Voice-reference draft; not committed game text.

speaker: Whimsy
text: Yes. The poster belongs with proportionality. Small act, enormous consequence.
notes: Voice-reference draft; not committed game text.

speaker: Whimsy
text: Good. Selective enforcement is the bruise under the rule.
notes: Voice-reference draft; not committed game text.

speaker: Whimsy
text: The poster matters, but it cannot testify about what the notice forgot to say.
notes: Voice-reference draft; not committed game text.

speaker: Whimsy
context: wrong mapping Crab evidence to proportionality
text: That is Crab's factual blade. Do not use it to butter proportionality.
notes: Voice-reference draft; not committed game text.

speaker: Whimsy
text: The board has become almost elegant. I fear for its reputation.
notes: Voice-reference draft; not committed game text.

speaker: Whimsy
text: This board is less an argument than a nervous collage. Still, miracles have entered on worse paperwork.
notes: Voice-reference draft; not committed game text.

speaker: Whimsy
text: Grzyb speaks for beige order. We speak for a home with a window.
notes: Voice-reference draft; not committed game text.

speaker: Whimsy
text: A rule must tell people what it requires before it punishes them for failing to guess.
notes: Voice-reference draft; not committed game text.

speaker: Whimsy
text: Rights exercised in beige are still rights. Though less festive.
notes: Voice-reference draft; not committed game text.

speaker: Whimsy
text: The alleged disturbance appears to have produced more paperwork than noise.
notes: Voice-reference draft; not committed game text.

speaker: Whimsy
text: Kowalski hears soup cooling in another room. If he missed the poster, the poster was innocent.
notes: Voice-reference draft; not committed game text.

speaker: Whimsy
text: Dates after the notice. The landlord has invented retrospective prophecy.
notes: Voice-reference draft; not committed game text.

speaker: Whimsy
text: The law may regulate buildings. It may not pretend a harmless poster weighs more than a home.
notes: Voice-reference draft; not committed game text.

speaker: Whimsy
text: A home is not a removable inconvenience when management dislikes the window.
notes: Voice-reference draft; not committed game text.

speaker: Whimsy
text: No. Do not argue artistic immunity. Even I have standards, though I conceal them theatrically.
notes: Voice-reference draft; not committed game text.

speaker: Whimsy
text: The judge has leaned forward. That is judicial applause, in a restrained habitat.
notes: Voice-reference draft; not committed game text.

speaker: Whimsy
text: The court wants documents. Feed it documents before it eats confidence.
notes: Voice-reference draft; not committed game text.

speaker: Whimsy
text: Waldek keeps the home. That is not a flourish. That is the point.
notes: Voice-reference draft; not committed game text.

speaker: Whimsy
text: Proportionality lives. I shall not say 'I told you so' until we are safely outside.
notes: Voice-reference draft; not committed game text.

speaker: Whimsy
text: Temporary safety is still safety. Tonight, the lock remains his.
notes: Voice-reference draft; not committed game text.

speaker: Whimsy
text: A second poster! The window has discovered solidarity.
notes: Voice-reference draft; not committed game text.

speaker: Whimsy
text: Kowalski supports rights, but not kerning. A nuanced man.
notes: Voice-reference draft; not committed game text.

speaker: Whimsy
text: I have designed a poster: PROPORTIONALITY LIVES HERE.
notes: Voice-reference draft; not committed game text.

speaker: Whimsy
text: Murrow says I cannot hang it in the window. The revolution is being reviewed for fire safety.
notes: Voice-reference draft; not committed game text.

speaker: Whimsy
text: The printer has issued a prophecy. I hate when office equipment develops narrative timing.
notes: Voice-reference draft; not committed game text.

speaker: Whimsy
text: One home is safe. The system will resent that. Systems are petty.
notes: Voice-reference draft; not committed game text.

speaker: Whimsy
text: Do not let Grzyb turn Waldek into an administrative example. He is a person, not a warning label.
notes: Voice-reference draft; not committed game text.

speaker: Whimsy
text: Careful, Dr. A. Cula. Grzyb's politeness is where he stores the knives.
notes: Voice-reference draft; not committed game text.

speaker: Whimsy
text: Look at you, arranging evidence like it has furniture. Growth.
notes: Voice-reference draft; not committed game text.

speaker: Whimsy
text: The printer screams because no one has ever asked what litigation does to machinery.
notes: Voice-reference draft; not committed game text.

speaker: Whimsy
text: I propose we treat the printer as a hostile witness with toner-based trauma.
notes: Voice-reference draft; not committed game text.

speaker: Whimsy
text: Murrow rejects my theory of procedural haunting. History will judge him, assuming it prints.
notes: Voice-reference draft; not committed game text.

speaker: Whimsy
text: Crab wants logs. I wanted an exorcism. Logs are cheaper.
notes: Voice-reference draft; not committed game text.

speaker: Whimsy
text: Bajtek arrives with the face of a man who has seen three routers lie under oath.
notes: Voice-reference draft; not committed game text.

speaker: Whimsy
text: The server room: where cables gather to discuss our weaknesses.
notes: Voice-reference draft; not committed game text.

speaker: Whimsy
text: Do not touch the blinking thing with confidence. Confidence is how technology eats fingers.
notes: Voice-reference draft; not committed game text.

speaker: Whimsy
text: He says printers do not become sentient. A brave position from someone standing inside their cathedral.
notes: Voice-reference draft; not committed game text.

speaker: Whimsy
text: Repeated redirection at the same time. Not chaos. Design.
notes: Voice-reference draft; not committed game text.

speaker: Whimsy
text: Contradiction spotting: the noble art of asking a statement why it has blood on its shoes.
notes: Voice-reference draft; not committed game text.

speaker: Whimsy
text: There. The log has spoken, and it has better memory than the witness.
notes: Voice-reference draft; not committed game text.

speaker: Whimsy
text: No. That evidence contradicts mostly our desire to be done.
notes: Voice-reference draft; not committed game text.

speaker: Whimsy
text: A print shop. The natural predator of small law firms.
notes: Voice-reference draft; not committed game text.

speaker: Whimsy
text: Pig & Swine's printer is a kettle compared to these iron bureaucrats.
notes: Voice-reference draft; not committed game text.

speaker: Whimsy
text: A rival firm paid for maintenance on our equipment. That is not coincidence; that is coincidence wearing gloves.
notes: Voice-reference draft; not committed game text.

speaker: Whimsy
text: Advocate Szpon enters like a deadline that learned tailoring.
notes: Voice-reference draft; not committed game text.

speaker: Whimsy
text: He denies connection with the exact temperature of someone standing near an invoice.
notes: Voice-reference draft; not committed game text.

speaker: Whimsy
text: The invoice, counsel. Paper remembers what predators prefer to forget.
notes: Voice-reference draft; not committed game text.

speaker: Whimsy
text: I dislike him professionally, aesthetically, and on behalf of punctuation.
notes: Voice-reference draft; not committed game text.

speaker: Whimsy
text: The basement smells of old files, wet cardboard, and claims not yet limitation-barred.
notes: Voice-reference draft; not committed game text.

speaker: Whimsy
text: A reroute device. So the printer was not cursed. Merely exploited. Somehow worse.
notes: Voice-reference draft; not committed game text.

speaker: Whimsy
text: A rival-firm leaflet in our basement. Subtle as a marching band in a witness box.
notes: Voice-reference draft; not committed game text.

speaker: Whimsy
text: This is no longer a printer problem. This is access to court with wires around its throat.
notes: Voice-reference draft; not committed game text.

speaker: Whimsy
text: Murrow prepares the filing. Crab watches the route. I have been banned from the cover page, unjustly.
notes: Voice-reference draft; not committed game text.

speaker: Whimsy
text: The emergency scooter has become a legal instrument. I always knew transport law had glamour.
notes: Voice-reference draft; not committed game text.

speaker: Whimsy
text: The filing arrived with dignity, velocity, and only minor wind-based jurisprudence.
notes: Voice-reference draft; not committed game text.

speaker: Whimsy
text: Tyre marks are not ideal, but they do prove movement before the deadline.
notes: Voice-reference draft; not committed game text.

speaker: Whimsy
text: The filing has suffered a pilgrimage. Let us call it character evidence.
notes: Voice-reference draft; not committed game text.

speaker: Whimsy
text: We stand before the court to defend a filing from murder by office equipment.
notes: Voice-reference draft; not committed game text.

speaker: Whimsy
text: Ordinary incompetence is random. This was punctual. Punctuality is where sabotage signs its name.
notes: Voice-reference draft; not committed game text.

speaker: Whimsy
text: Bajtek will say the technical thing. I will resist making it mythological.
notes: Voice-reference draft; not committed game text.

speaker: Whimsy
text: The rival firm appears exactly where innocence would prefer a blank space.
notes: Voice-reference draft; not committed game text.

speaker: Whimsy
text: Szpon calls it melodrama because the invoice has stolen his calm.
notes: Voice-reference draft; not committed game text.

speaker: Whimsy
text: A deadline cannot become a weapon for the person secretly moving the clock.
notes: Voice-reference draft; not committed game text.

speaker: Whimsy
text: The court should accept the filing because process sabotaged from the shadows is not process at all.
notes: Voice-reference draft; not committed game text.

speaker: Whimsy
text: We waved the wrong document at the right lie. Tragic choreography.
notes: Voice-reference draft; not committed game text.

speaker: Whimsy
text: The lie has cracked. Please admire the fracture responsibly.
notes: Voice-reference draft; not committed game text.

speaker: Whimsy
text: The court wants causation. Give it logs, invoices, and a clean path between them.
notes: Voice-reference draft; not committed game text.

speaker: Whimsy
text: The filing lives. So does the client's chance to be heard.
notes: Voice-reference draft; not committed game text.

speaker: Whimsy
text: The printer is exonerated. I hope everyone who called it haunted feels procedurally ashamed.
notes: Voice-reference draft; not committed game text.

speaker: Whimsy
text: Printer, noble beast, we doubted you. Mostly Pig doubted you. I composed a shorter apology.
notes: Voice-reference draft; not committed game text.

speaker: Whimsy
text: It printed THANK YOU. Either forgiveness exists, or toner is manipulating us.
notes: Voice-reference draft; not committed game text.

speaker: Whimsy
text: And now OUT OF CYAN. Forgiveness has limits.
notes: Voice-reference draft; not committed game text.

speaker: Whimsy
text: Szpon retreats, but not as a defeated man. As a man saving malice for later.
notes: Voice-reference draft; not committed game text.

speaker: Whimsy
text: A postcard with a bill attached. Swine has invented a new genre of threat.
notes: Voice-reference draft; not committed game text.

speaker: Whimsy
text: Let Bajtek work. Printer mysticism is my burden, not his.
notes: Voice-reference draft; not committed game text.

speaker: Whimsy
text: Dr. A. Cula, keep the line simple: abnormal pattern, rival link, procedural prejudice. No thunder until remedy.
notes: Voice-reference draft; not committed game text.

speaker: Whimsy
text: Provisionally accepted is still accepted. Process has a pulse.
notes: Voice-reference draft; not committed game text.

speaker: Whimsy
text: We have defeated sabotage by becoming more organised than our office suggests. An unsettling precedent.
notes: Voice-reference draft; not committed game text.

speaker: Whimsy
text: International arbitration. At last, Pig & Swine has become expensive in multiple currencies.
notes: Voice-reference draft; not committed game text.

speaker: Whimsy
text: Mr. Swine returns wearing the expression of a man chased through customs by consequences.
notes: Voice-reference draft; not committed game text.

speaker: Whimsy
text: When Swine says 'mostly cultural,' check the invoice for teeth.
notes: Voice-reference draft; not committed game text.

speaker: Whimsy
text: Pig, breathe. Then shout. Then breathe again. We need the facts before the opera.
notes: Voice-reference draft; not committed game text.

speaker: Whimsy
text: Mr. Swine, please place your memories in chronological order, or at least stop letting them ski downhill.
notes: Voice-reference draft; not committed game text.

speaker: Whimsy
text: He is answering around the question with impressive international footwork.
notes: Voice-reference draft; not committed game text.

speaker: Whimsy
text: Warsaw Airport: where luggage, declarations, and poor decisions emerge on belts.
notes: Voice-reference draft; not committed game text.

speaker: Whimsy
text: The customs form says no commercial intent. Even bureaucracy occasionally tells the truth by accident.
notes: Voice-reference draft; not committed game text.

speaker: Whimsy
text: Swine's luggage has generated more procedural history than some cases.
notes: Voice-reference draft; not committed game text.

speaker: Whimsy
text: The arrival board contradicts the invoice timeline. Public transport becomes witness testimony.
notes: Voice-reference draft; not committed game text.

speaker: Whimsy
text: The clerk has seen many suitcases. Few have looked this liable.
notes: Voice-reference draft; not committed game text.

speaker: Whimsy
text: We behave here. The comedy target is Swine, not translation, not hospitality, not Japan.
notes: Voice-reference draft; not committed game text.

speaker: Whimsy
text: Ms. Tanaka has the calm of someone about to rescue a contract from Swine's enthusiasm.
notes: Voice-reference draft; not committed game text.

speaker: Whimsy
text: There is a world of difference between courteous cooperation and a binding invoice with claws.
notes: Voice-reference draft; not committed game text.

speaker: Whimsy
text: Politeness wearing an expensive hat. I may have to embroider that on Swine's defence.
notes: Voice-reference draft; not committed game text.

speaker: Whimsy
text: An appointment log: the diary of institutions that still believe in sequence.
notes: Voice-reference draft; not committed game text.

speaker: Whimsy
text: No easy jokes here. The absurdity belongs to the man who signed menus under stress.
notes: Voice-reference draft; not committed game text.

speaker: Whimsy
text: Swine's apartment has the financial confidence of a man who has never met Mr. Pig's ledger.
notes: Voice-reference draft; not committed game text.

speaker: Whimsy
text: This furniture knows things. Expensive things. Possibly depreciable things.
notes: Voice-reference draft; not committed game text.

speaker: Whimsy
text: The expense report appears to have been assembled during turbulence and moral hesitation.
notes: Voice-reference draft; not committed game text.

speaker: Whimsy
text: The draft contains no binding payment clause. Someone added fear in a different font.
notes: Voice-reference draft; not committed game text.

speaker: Whimsy
text: This is not luxury spending. This is a quiet rescue wearing a terrible label.
notes: Voice-reference draft; not committed game text.

speaker: Whimsy
text: He is hiding something, but not all hidden things are knives.
notes: Voice-reference draft; not committed game text.

speaker: Whimsy
text: We now descend into Mr. Swine's memory, which appears to have poor traction and several consultancy obstacles.
notes: Voice-reference draft; not committed game text.

speaker: Whimsy
text: At last, a memory with edges. He signed a welcome memorandum, not a commercial curse.
notes: Voice-reference draft; not committed game text.

speaker: Whimsy
text: Snow, panic, clipboard, pudding. Not ideal, but legally aromatic.
notes: Voice-reference draft; not committed game text.

speaker: Whimsy
text: The memory has become mostly downhill weather. We proceed with documents and prayer.
notes: Voice-reference draft; not committed game text.

speaker: Whimsy
text: Dr. A. Cula handles law. Swine handles facts, memories, and edible exhibits.
notes: Voice-reference draft; not committed game text.

speaker: Whimsy
text: Do not let Swine interpret the contract unless the tribunal asks how it felt in his hands.
notes: Voice-reference draft; not committed game text.

speaker: Whimsy
text: Law on one side, absurd facts on the other. Balance, in the tragic sense.
notes: Voice-reference draft; not committed game text.

speaker: Whimsy
text: The tribunal will hear submissions, not ski confessions. A loss for theatre, a gain for survival.
notes: Voice-reference draft; not committed game text.

speaker: Whimsy
text: Courtesy is not consideration. A toast is not a payment clause.
notes: Voice-reference draft; not committed game text.

speaker: Whimsy
text: The claimant asks politeness to carry a debt. Politeness is not built for that burden.
notes: Voice-reference draft; not committed game text.

speaker: Whimsy
text: Travel, hospitality, and confusion do not bind the firm without authority.
notes: Voice-reference draft; not committed game text.

speaker: Whimsy
text: Swine had authority over his skis. Let us not overstate the mandate.
notes: Voice-reference draft; not committed game text.

speaker: Whimsy
text: Seventeen puddings is not an invoice line. It is a cry for governance.
notes: Voice-reference draft; not committed game text.

speaker: Whimsy
text: Good. Let Swine explain the puddings. No one else should be burdened with that knowledge.
notes: Voice-reference draft; not committed game text.

speaker: Whimsy
text: The tribunal need not canonise Swine. It need only reject the obligation not proven.
notes: Voice-reference draft; not committed game text.

speaker: Whimsy
text: Pig & Swine is chaotic, but chaos is not contractual assent.
notes: Voice-reference draft; not committed game text.

speaker: Whimsy
text: That evidence has wandered into the wrong country.
notes: Voice-reference draft; not committed game text.

speaker: Whimsy
text: The claimant's theory is wobbling like Swine on rented skis.
notes: Voice-reference draft; not committed game text.

speaker: Whimsy
text: International hambitration has been survived. I refuse to apologise for that word.
notes: Voice-reference draft; not committed game text.

speaker: Whimsy
text: The firm is not liable. Swine's dignity may owe a small personal sum.
notes: Voice-reference draft; not committed game text.

speaker: Whimsy
text: Alive, invoiced, and technically successful. Pig & Swine's natural habitat.
notes: Voice-reference draft; not committed game text.

speaker: Whimsy
text: Some of those strange accounts kept clients alive. That changes the colour of the mess.
notes: Voice-reference draft; not committed game text.

speaker: Whimsy
text: Do not reduce him to the ski goggles. A fool can still do one brave thing badly.
notes: Voice-reference draft; not committed game text.

speaker: Whimsy
text: A mass claim threatens the district. Of course. The city has seen our chaos and decided to scale it.
notes: Voice-reference draft; not committed game text.

speaker: Whimsy
text: That was nearly an apology. I saw sincerity pass through, frightened but alive.
notes: Voice-reference draft; not committed game text.

speaker: Whimsy
text: Steady, Dr. A. Cula. Wisdom is not the issue. Proof is.
notes: Voice-reference draft; not committed game text.

speaker: Whimsy
text: Ms. Tanaka has reduced Swine's chaos to grammar. I may applaud quietly.
notes: Voice-reference draft; not committed game text.

speaker: Whimsy
text: The district has been served. Apparently bureaucracy has discovered area damage.
notes: Voice-reference draft; not committed game text.

speaker: Whimsy
text: They are not closing rooms. They are closing doors people use when other doors reject them.
notes: Voice-reference draft; not committed game text.

speaker: Whimsy
text: Pig is trying to interpret a closure notice optimistically. Someone take away his maritime charts.
notes: Voice-reference draft; not committed game text.

speaker: Whimsy
text: Generic notices, individual consequences. That is the wound.
notes: Voice-reference draft; not committed game text.

speaker: Whimsy
text: Asia, stop apologising for the apocalypse. You did not schedule this one.
notes: Voice-reference draft; not committed game text.

speaker: Whimsy
text: This is what an administrative scheme looks like after it leaves the printer: faces, bags, children, rain.
notes: Voice-reference draft; not committed game text.

speaker: Whimsy
text: Impact statements are not sentimental extras. They are how consequences become visible to power.
notes: Voice-reference draft; not committed game text.

speaker: Whimsy
text: Waldek was not an exception. He was a preview.
notes: Voice-reference draft; not committed game text.

speaker: Whimsy
text: Kowalski has appealed himself twice and reached a provisional settlement with his mailbox.
notes: Voice-reference draft; not committed game text.

speaker: Whimsy
text: Zielińska sees the pattern: everyone treated the same because no one was seen at all.
notes: Voice-reference draft; not committed game text.

speaker: Whimsy
text: Even Café Paragraf is threatened. That is not redevelopment; that is an attack on procedural caffeine.
notes: Voice-reference draft; not committed game text.

speaker: Whimsy
text: Grzyb says he enjoys rules and therefore objects. I am frightened by how much I respect this sentence.
notes: Voice-reference draft; not committed game text.

speaker: Whimsy
text: We have the human harm. Now find the machine that pretended not to see it.
notes: Voice-reference draft; not committed game text.

speaker: Whimsy
text: City Hall: where sentences go to become passive voice and public interest.
notes: Voice-reference draft; not committed game text.

speaker: Whimsy
text: It says access to justice while removing the people currently providing it. A sentence with a shovel.
notes: Voice-reference draft; not committed game text.

speaker: Whimsy
text: Identical notices for different lives. Efficiency has eaten individual assessment.
notes: Voice-reference draft; not committed game text.

speaker: Whimsy
text: The money trail has arrived wearing a city badge.
notes: Voice-reference draft; not committed game text.

speaker: Whimsy
text: Each case individually assessed? Then every individual has the same printer settings. Touching unity.
notes: Voice-reference draft; not committed game text.

speaker: Whimsy
text: The template batch has spoken. It says the assessment was copy-paste with a tie.
notes: Voice-reference draft; not committed game text.

speaker: Whimsy
text: That document accuses the wrong hypocrisy. We have many; choose carefully.
notes: Voice-reference draft; not committed game text.

speaker: Whimsy
text: Behold the industrial printer: bureaucracy with a jaw.
notes: Voice-reference draft; not committed game text.

speaker: Whimsy
text: Objections marked duplicate before review. That is not administration. That is silencing with columns.
notes: Voice-reference draft; not committed game text.

speaker: Whimsy
text: One command, hundreds of consequences. Automation did not remove discretion; it hid the hand.
notes: Voice-reference draft; not committed game text.

speaker: Whimsy
text: Bajtek is speaking calmly, which means the system is worse than screaming would suggest.
notes: Voice-reference draft; not committed game text.

speaker: Whimsy
text: The final printer rises. I knew paper would betray civilisation eventually.
notes: Voice-reference draft; not committed game text.

speaker: Whimsy
text: Dodge the notices! They are legally harmful and poorly justified.
notes: Voice-reference draft; not committed game text.

speaker: Whimsy
text: Stamped! Emergency procedure meets office athletics.
notes: Voice-reference draft; not committed game text.

speaker: Whimsy
text: Avoid the toner cloud. It contains both cyan and despair.
notes: Voice-reference draft; not committed game text.

speaker: Whimsy
text: The beast is stopped. The objections live. Somewhere, a filing cabinet weeps.
notes: Voice-reference draft; not committed game text.

speaker: Whimsy
text: Messy, but stopped. Most institutions would call that implementation.
notes: Voice-reference draft; not committed game text.

speaker: Whimsy
text: Bajtek has saved us from total paper extinction. The logs are singed but legible enough to accuse.
notes: Voice-reference draft; not committed game text.

speaker: Whimsy
text: The ledger is not vanity. It is a map of who could enter the office because Swine secretly paid the light bill.
notes: Voice-reference draft; not committed game text.

speaker: Whimsy
text: He hid generosity under nonsense because sincerity frightened him more than arbitration.
notes: Voice-reference draft; not committed game text.

speaker: Whimsy
text: Pig is experiencing gratitude, betrayal, and accountancy at once. Clear the room.
notes: Voice-reference draft; not committed game text.

speaker: Whimsy
text: He should have told us. Also, people were helped. Both things are true.
notes: Voice-reference draft; not committed game text.

speaker: Whimsy
text: The Final Brief board. Every friend becomes an argument; every argument needs a friend.
notes: Voice-reference draft; not committed game text.

speaker: Whimsy
text: Good. Proportionality requires language that refuses to let people become footnotes.
notes: Voice-reference draft; not committed game text.

speaker: Whimsy
text: Closing argument? At last, thunder with supervision.
notes: Voice-reference draft; not committed game text.

speaker: Whimsy
text: I can describe the algorithm as a dragon wearing a spreadsheet, but Bajtek may be more helpful.
notes: Voice-reference draft; not committed game text.

speaker: Whimsy
text: Procurement is Crab's swamp. I enter only with a rope and a metaphor.
notes: Voice-reference draft; not committed game text.

speaker: Whimsy
text: Do not assign Pig to conflict of interest unless the conflict is between panic and furniture.
notes: Voice-reference draft; not committed game text.

speaker: Whimsy
text: Asia is not on the witness list because the court cannot process how much she has already saved.
notes: Voice-reference draft; not committed game text.

speaker: Whimsy
text: This brief has structure, evidence, witnesses, and one controlled flame. I approve.
notes: Voice-reference draft; not committed game text.

speaker: Whimsy
text: This brief has courage and several loose beams. We may yet call it architecture.
notes: Voice-reference draft; not committed game text.

speaker: Whimsy
text: The Supreme Court. Even my metaphors have put on formal shoes.
notes: Voice-reference draft; not committed game text.

speaker: Whimsy
text: A notice no ordinary person can understand is not notice. It is decoration with consequences.
notes: Voice-reference draft; not committed game text.

speaker: Whimsy
text: Kowalski read the notice six times and accidentally appealed his hallway. That is not clarity.
notes: Voice-reference draft; not committed game text.

speaker: Whimsy
text: Public interest is not a spell that makes individual lives disappear.
notes: Voice-reference draft; not committed game text.

speaker: Whimsy
text: The scheme speaks in blocks and zones. The evidence answers in homes, clients, and closed doors.
notes: Voice-reference draft; not committed game text.

speaker: Whimsy
text: Automation did not remove discretion. It hid discretion where objections went to die.
notes: Voice-reference draft; not committed game text.

speaker: Whimsy
text: Bajtek says the folder was called 'Not Helpful.' Even villainy has naming conventions.
notes: Voice-reference draft; not committed game text.

speaker: Whimsy
text: The same system that printed the notices profits when the noticed people disappear.
notes: Voice-reference draft; not committed game text.

speaker: Whimsy
text: When Grzyb blushes at procedure, procedure has fallen very far.
notes: Voice-reference draft; not committed game text.

speaker: Whimsy
text: Modernisation may improve a city. It may not begin by silencing the people most likely to need law.
notes: Voice-reference draft; not committed game text.

speaker: Whimsy
text: Pig & Swine is absurd. That is partly why desperate people can enter without feeling judged by marble.
notes: Voice-reference draft; not committed game text.

speaker: Whimsy
text: The remedy is not to freeze the city forever. It is to stop the machine long enough for people to be counted.
notes: Voice-reference draft; not committed game text.

speaker: Whimsy
text: That submission has mistaken volume for weight. A common tragedy in final hearings.
notes: Voice-reference draft; not committed game text.

speaker: Whimsy
text: The evidence lands. Even marble has ears, apparently.
notes: Voice-reference draft; not committed game text.

speaker: Whimsy
text: The Court is not hostile. It is testing whether our outrage has documents.
notes: Voice-reference draft; not committed game text.

speaker: Whimsy
text: Szpon calls disappearance modernisation. Predators do adore abstract nouns.
notes: Voice-reference draft; not committed game text.

speaker: Whimsy
text: No, counsel. Efficiency that erases objections is not efficiency. It is concealment with a dashboard.
notes: Voice-reference draft; not committed game text.

speaker: Whimsy
text: The district breathes. I shall be insufferable for several days.
notes: Voice-reference draft; not committed game text.

speaker: Whimsy
text: The scheme is suspended. Doors remain open while the city learns to count people properly.
notes: Voice-reference draft; not committed game text.

speaker: Whimsy
text: A temporary injunction is still a hand against the closing door.
notes: Voice-reference draft; not committed game text.

speaker: Whimsy
text: The office remains shabby. Good. Sudden respectability would damage the plumbing.
notes: Voice-reference draft; not committed game text.

speaker: Whimsy
text: Asia, if anyone deserves a statue, it is you. Unfortunately, our budget allows one labelled mug.
notes: Voice-reference draft; not committed game text.

speaker: Whimsy
text: Crab is pleased. Look closely; the disappointment has softened by almost one millimetre.
notes: Voice-reference draft; not committed game text.

speaker: Whimsy
text: Pig is preparing remarks. I have alerted the coast guard and the calendar.
notes: Voice-reference draft; not committed game text.

speaker: Whimsy
text: Swine is still evasive. But now, when he hides a thing, we check whether it is kindness before accusing it of fraud.
notes: Voice-reference draft; not committed game text.

speaker: Whimsy
text: Waldek's poster has multiplied again. The wall has chosen jurisprudence.
notes: Voice-reference draft; not committed game text.

speaker: Whimsy
text: Grzyb sent a beige congratulatory card. I am oddly moved and visually underwhelmed.
notes: Voice-reference draft; not committed game text.

speaker: Whimsy
text: WELCOME BACK, COUNSEL. The printer has become sentimental. This is how sequels begin.
notes: Voice-reference draft; not committed game text.

speaker: Whimsy
text: LOW PAPER. Even victory has supply-chain objections.
notes: Voice-reference draft; not committed game text.

speaker: Whimsy
text: It lets people through the door. That is not glamorous. That is why it matters.
notes: Voice-reference draft; not committed game text.

speaker: Whimsy
text: Dr. A. Cula, close simply. The case is big; the point is not. Let people through the door.
notes: Voice-reference draft; not committed game text.

speaker: Whimsy
text: No flourishes for this one. The final argument must be sharp enough to cut paper, not merely decorate it.
notes: Voice-reference draft; not committed game text.

speaker: Whimsy
text: No jokes at their expense. They already paid the price of the city's efficiency.
notes: Voice-reference draft; not committed game text.

speaker: Whimsy
text: 'Continuing legal-social utility' is how bureaucracy says 'prove you are human enough to remain indoors.'
notes: Voice-reference draft; not committed game text.

speaker: Whimsy
text: Our old printer tried to warn us. Naturally, it used cyan and trauma.
notes: Voice-reference draft; not committed game text.

speaker: Whimsy
text: Suspend the scheme. Restore the objections. Require individual assessment. Let the doors stay open while law remembers its users.
notes: Voice-reference draft; not committed game text.
